trails.name: KETEMARINGI Route
trails.description: Climb to the Ketemaringi Trig.

trails.name: WAIPAPA LOOP TRACK
trails.description: This short loop walk in the Waipapa ecological area takes you through tall podocarp with spectacular bird life.

trails.name: TE MAIRE LOOP TRACK
trails.description: Walk through a fine example of the podocarp forest that once covered much of the central North Island. It's a suitable track for kids.

trails.name: WAIHORA RD,LAGOON TK JUNCTN TO WERAROA T
ARATAKI TRACK
trails.description: This track starts with a suspension bridge crossing over the Mangakino stream. You'll get to see an abundant of birdlife towards the base of Mt Titiraupenga.

trails.name: MAPARA TRACK
trails.description: You can hear and sometimes see kōkako and other birds on the Mapara Track.

trails.name: LINK RD CRPK TO CN ISLAND TK JUNCTN
CENT NTH ISL TK JUCT TO MT TITIRAUPENGA
Hihikiwi Track
trails.description: Hihikiwi Track is the shortest track to Pāhautea Hut and Pirongia summit. Walk to the lookout point on Mount Pirongia for one of the best vantage points over the west coast harbours. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Timber Trail Piropiro rd to Maramataha
Timber Trail to Bog Inn Track  (North)
Hihikiwi Lookout
trails.description: Walk to this lookout point on Mount Pirongia for one of the best vantage points over the west coast harbours.

trails.name: Kakepuku Track
trails.description: The Kakepuku Track in Kakepuku Historic Reserve climbs up through bush to the summit of Kakepuku, an old volcano.

trails.name: Kawakawa Bay tracks
trails.description: The K2K and Orakau tracks both take you into the picturesque Kawakawa Bay; walk or ride the trails separately, or link them together for a longer day out.

trails.name: Ohinetonga Track
trails.description: Walk through a variety of attractive scenery - with picnic areas and good places to swim as well this track is a good option with children.

trails.name: Omaru Falls Walk
trails.description: A gentle 30 min walk through bush and over farmland to the beautiful Omaru Falls. On Omaru Road, clearly signposted from SH4.

trails.name: Opapaka Pā Walk
trails.description: Opapaka Pā Walk is closed due to a large slip.

trails.name: Pirongia summit via Hihikiwi Track
trails.description: Take the shortest track to Pāhautea Hut and Pirongia summit.

trails.name: Pukawa Bay walks
trails.description: Walk through ancient forest, experience spectacular lake vistas and enjoy constant birdsong on a variety of walks on the southwestern shores of Lake Taupō.

trails.name: Ruakuri Walk
trails.description: Discover a forested gorge, natural sculpted cliffs and low limestone arches on this spectacular loop walk - plenty to keep kids interested.

trails.name: Tongariro River Trail
trails.description: Follow the banks of the Tongariro River alongside farmland, through native bush and urban areas, enjoying magnificent river views. An easy, mainly flat track with multiple entry points - a great family friendly option.

trails.name: Waihaha and Waihora tracks
trails.description: Explore the remote bush, cliffs and waterfalls of the Western Bays of Lake Taupō. Normally accessible only by boat, these trails take you through a variety of landscapes with fantastic lookout points.

trails.name: Waitomo Walkway
trails.description: The Waitomo Walkway traverses a classic limestone landscape and meanders beside the Waitomo Stream through forest and open farmland. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Whakaipo Bay tracks
trails.description: Walk or ride over the headland between Whakaipo and Whangamata bays and enjoy the lake views and native bush.

trails.name: Rimu Walk
trails.description: This short loop walk from Kakaho Campsite takes in the fantastic views and the tall trees of the Pureora Forest.

trails.name: Waihora Lagoon Walk
trails.description: This short walk leads to a viewing platform over a lagoon, where tall trees are reflected in the water.

trails.name: Mount Pureora from Link Road
trails.description: On a clear day, enjoy views across Lake Taupo, the Mamaku Ranges, Mount Taranaki and Mount Ruapehu from the summit of Mount Pureora.

trails.name: Mount Pureora via Toitoi Track
trails.description: Walk to the summit of Mount Pureora from The Timber Trail. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Centre of the North Island and Mount Titiraupenga tracks
trails.description: Take a short walk to the Centre of the North Island or climb Mount Titiraupenga.

trails.name: Waihaha Hut Track
trails.description: Walk to Waihaha Hut for a short overnight trip.

trails.name: Kakepuku
trails.description: The Kakepuku Track climbs up through bush to the summit of Kakepuku, an old volcano. Part of the Te Ara Wai Journeys sites.

trails.name: Kihikihi Cycleway
trails.description: An easy track suitable for walking and cycling which extends from Te Awamutu to Kihikihi. On-street paths between the two towns.

trails.name: Maungatautari   Nikau Track
trails.description: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island and wildlife sanctuary. This track is paid entry.

trails.name: Maungatautari   Over the Mountain Track
trails.description: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island and wildlife sanctuary. This track is not suitable for children under 12 years of age. Suitable for experienced hikers and adventurous walkers with a good level of fitness

trails.name: Maungatautari   Rata Track
trails.description: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island and wildlife sanctuary. This track is not suitable for children under 12 years of age. Suitable for experienced hikers and adventurous walkers with a good level of fitness

trails.name: Maungatautari   Rimu Track
trails.description: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island and wildlife sanctuary. This track is paid entry.

trails.name: Maungatautari   Te Aria Tirohia
trails.description: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island and wildlife sanctuary.

trails.name: Pioneer Walk
trails.description: An easy to moderate walk, some parts are gravel and some parts are steep terrain. Track follows the Mangahoi Stream through park reserve and has sections of on-street footpaths.

trails.name: Timber Trail Connection (Ongarue to Taumaranu)
trails.description: NI Heartland Rides

trails.name: Rangaroa Reserve Walk
trails.description: There are picnic areas and good places to swim by the river.

trails.name: Sunshine Track
trails.description: This is not a very long walk but it is really lovely and worth the effort. Cross Victory Bridge and turn left down Marsack Road and the entrance to the walk is�sign posted on the right. The narrow drive leads to a small car park with a wooden fence and
